message:
fix(test): mcp_manifest_golden whitelist asserts tool still exists before no-db check (ft-cbeyl)

The three `expected_absent_*` whitelists in
`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db` only asserted
`!no_db.contains(X)`. If a whitelisted tool/resource/template was
renamed or removed from the db-backed manifest, the check passed
trivially (the name disappears from both sets and `!contains` stays
true), silently masking any regression in tool gating.

Add a `full_*.contains(X)` pre-assert for each whitelist entry so the
guard remains meaningful: if the whitelisted identifier disappears
from the db-backed manifest, the test fails and forces a deliberate
whitelist update.

Verified: 6/6 tests pass under
`rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mcp_manifest_golden
--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime`.
